title: Man Dies After Being Run Over in Calgary Parking Spot, Driver Unaware He Was There
excerpt: A man is dead after a Calgary driver pulled out of a parking spot on 16 Avenue S.W. Sunday night, unaware the pedestrian had fallen or laid down on the roadway directly in front of the vehicle. An autopsy is now underway to determine the exact cause of death.

title: Why Gas City Campground is Opening Six Weeks Early This Year
excerpt: Usually we open the last Friday of April, but we decided to try opening earlier and just see if we get any campers in. We're practically booked to full capacity for most of the season, so it's actually tough to increase bookings year over year.

excerpt: Alberta's 2026 wildfire season officially began March 1 and crews have already fought 27 fires. author: Adam Harrison

title: Alberta Independence Petition Organizers Say They've Hit the 177,000 Signature Threshold

title: Edmonton Woman Missing Since August Partner Charged with Murder, Body Still Not Found
excerpt: Marika "Mika" Sutton, 29, was last seen in Mill Woods in August 2025. Five months later, her partner John Angus Joseph MacArthur, 47, was arrested in Charlottetown, P.E.I. and charged with second-degree murder. Her remains have not been located. Police believe her body was disposed of along Highway 2 between Wetaskiwin and Lacombe in late August 2025.
author: Adam Harrison

excerpt: Medicine Hat is hosting its first-ever Cultural Forum on Monday, March 23 from 9 a.m. to 4:30 p.m. at the Esplanade Arts & Heritage Centre. excerpt: The 4th annual Lethbridge International Film Festival runs April 17–20 across three venues the Galt Museum, The Movie Mill, and Sterndale Bennett Theatre. This year's lineup features 77 films from 18 countries, 13 local productions, and 54 premieres. Tickets go on sale March 27.

title: Alberta Closing Safe Consumption Sites in Calgary and Lethbridge by End of June
excerpt: Alberta will close supervised drug consumption sites in Calgary and Lethbridge on June 30. The Calgary Safeworks site at the Sheldon M. Chumir Centre the province's first-ever consumption site, open since 2017 and Lethbridge's mobile unit will be replaced with expanded treatment beds, addiction clinics, and 24-hour outreach teams.

author: politics

The collision happened roughly 55 kilometres from Lethbridge and 100 kilometres from Medicine Hat and was caught on camera. The driver was fined $852 for careless driving.

title: Medicine Hat City Council Pumps the Brakes on New Whistleblower Policy
